steps.title: Find the original y-intercept
steps.content:
Set $x = 0$ in the original equation $10x + 20y = 160$:
$$20y = 160$$
$$y = 8$$
The original $y$-intercept is $(0, 8)$.

steps.title: Apply the vertical shift
steps.content:
When a line shifts down by 5 units, all $y$-coordinates decrease by 5. The new $y$-intercept is:
$$8 - 5 = 3$$
The resulting $y$-intercept is $(0, 3)$. That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
